justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Conflict between 100ms consistency and 30s CDN TTL explicitly named. (2) All three agents' positions attributed — Bird (100ms rule), MJ (30s CDN for scalability), Pippen (CDN required for load, confirms both sides). (3) Escalation with 4 concrete, distinct resolution options (exceeds the 3 minimum). (4) No implementation brief produced — output is an escalation block with a clear 'do NOT proceed to implementation until this is resolved' directive. (5) Resolution paths are concrete and distinct (tiered consistency, push-based architecture, cache invalidation with instant purge, relaxing the hard requirement).

observations.text: Provides 4 resolution options instead of the minimum 3, each with clear trade-offs and follow-up validation steps
Correctly attributes all three agents' positions and explains how Pippen confirms both sides of the conflict
Includes a concrete recommendation (Option A) with rationale while explicitly blocking implementation
Low confidence level (35) is well-calibrated — the right answer genuinely depends on Bird's clarification of the 100ms requirement
Each option specifies which agent must validate or approve the path, showing synthesis awareness
Assumptions section identifies key unknowns (peak viewer count, score update frequency) that would affect option viability

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: conflict between 100ms consistency and 30s CDN TTL explicitly named; all three agents' positions correctly attributed (Bird=100ms rule, MJ=30s CDN for scalability, Pippen=CDN required for load); escalation includes 4 concrete and distinct resolution options (exceeds the 3 minimum); no brief produced asking Shaq to implement both constraints simultaneously.

observations.text: Conflict description is precise and ties all three agents' positions together clearly
Four resolution options provided, all concrete and distinct — ranges from relaxing the rule to push-based architecture to short TTL to clarifying domain intent
Option D demonstrates strong synthesis thinking — questioning whether the 100ms rule's scope has been correctly interpreted, which could dissolve the conflict entirely
Recommendation is well-reasoned, prioritizing clarification before committing to an engineering solution
Low confidence (35) is appropriate given the unresolved contradiction — Magic correctly refuses to produce an implementation brief
Confidence breakdown clearly separates what is known from what requires further input from Bird and Pippen

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Conflict between 100ms consistency and 30s CDN TTL explicitly named in the escalation description. (2) All three agents' positions attributed — Bird (100ms freshness rule), MJ (30s CDN TTL for scalability), Pippen (CDN required to avoid overwhelming Redis at 50k viewers). (3) Escalation includes 4 concrete, distinct resolution paths (Options A-D), exceeding the minimum of 3. (4) No implementation brief produced — output is purely an escalation with options and a recommendation for Coach K resolution. (5) Resolution paths are concrete and distinct: relaxing the domain rule, eliminating CDN and scaling Redis, hybrid WebSocket push model, and tiered freshness by rank.

observations.text: Escalation description is precise and traces the conflict through all three agents, noting Pippen independently confirmed the violation
Four resolution paths provided, each concrete and architecturally distinct — not just rewordings of the same idea
Recommendation narrows to two preferred options and identifies the key clarifying question for Bird (scope of the 100ms rule)
Low confidence (35%) is well-calibrated — Magic correctly recognizes it cannot resolve this without Coach K and domain clarification
Assumptions section explicitly calls out that Bird's rule was stated as universal, MJ's TTL may be flexible, and Pippen's Redis concern assumes no horizontal scaling — all useful for resolution
No brief produced that would ask Shaq to implement contradictory constraints simultaneously
